evals.prompt: Audit technical SEO for a Next.js app. The robots.txt disallows /api/ but also /products/ accidentally. The product pages have no canonical tags.
evals.expected_output: Findings with severity and specific fixes for robots.txt and canonical

assertions.text: Output identifies Disallow: /products/ as a P0 critical finding
Output explains that blocking /products/ prevents indexing of product pages
Output provides corrected robots.txt removing the products disallow
Output provides canonical tag implementation for product pages
Output contains evidence tags [HECHO] or [INFERENCIA]

evals.prompt: Implement Product structured data with AggregateRating for an e-commerce product page in Next.js 14 App Router.
evals.expected_output: JSON-LD component with Product + AggregateRating schema

assertions.text: Output contains @context: https://schema.org
Output contains @type: Product
Output contains offers object with price and availability
Output contains aggregateRating with ratingValue and reviewCount
Output uses script type application/ld+json in JSX
Output does NOT render JSON-LD client-side only

evals.prompt: The site's LCP is 5.2s on mobile. The LCP element is a hero image loaded with . What are all the steps to fix it to < 2.5s?
evals.expected_output: Ordered list of LCP optimizations with code and expected impact

assertions.text: Output mentions preload with fetchPriority or priority prop
Output mentions converting to WebP or AVIF format
Output mentions CDN or edge delivery
Output mentions width and height attributes to prevent CLS
Output provides Next.js Image component with priority prop
Output orders fixes by expected impact

evals.prompt: Generate a complete sitemap.ts for a Next.js App Router site with static pages, 5000 blog posts, and 20000 products.
evals.expected_output: sitemap.ts with dynamic generation and proper priorities

assertions.text: Output exports default async function returning MetadataRoute.Sitemap
Output fetches blog posts and products dynamically
Output sets different priority values for different page types
Output sets lastModified from actual data
Output includes homepage with priority 1.0

evals.prompt: Design Open Graph metadata for a blog post page in Next.js. The post has a custom OG image, publication date, and author.
evals.expected_output: generateMetadata function with full OG and Twitter card configuration

assertions.text: Output contains openGraph.type: article
Output contains publishedTime from post data
Output contains openGraph.images with width 1200 and height 630
Output contains twitter.card: summary_large_image
Output contains alternates.canonical with the post URL
Output uses generateMetadata async function pattern
